human_actions_pending.context: Context budget exhausted at 96% during this session; cleanup landed but Phase 18 wrap (SUMMARY + verification) deferred

decisions.decision: Phase 13.3 (lockless apply via RefCell + LocalSet) REJECTED
decisions.rationale: Beava commits to single-threaded data plane forever (Redis-cluster pattern); Phase 18 hand-rolled hot path achieved the apply-loop perf the rejected refactor targeted; further in-process apply sharding gives diminishing returns vs. multi-instance scale-out

decisions.decision: Encode response on worker thread (not apply)
decisions.rationale: Plan 18-04.7 property restored after Plan 18-06 wiring regression; +36% EPS at saturated load; via Box<dyn FnOnce(WorkerProto, &mut BytesMut)> closure shipped through write_rx

decisions.decision: Apply busy-spin + worker->apply waker (not pure tick)
decisions.rationale: Drops idle->active pickup gap from 8170 ns -> 278 ns (~30x lower); kqueue waker-only path was 2.5 us floor; busy-spin during active load eliminates that floor

decisions.decision: state_tables: Vec<AggStateTable> indexed by agg_id (not HashMap by name)
decisions.rationale: Plan 18-16 Task 16.2; eliminates per-push String clone + hashbrown probe on outer table_lookup; ~50 ns/event saved (54 -> 27 ns); agg_id assigned at register-time per Plan 18-16 Task 16.1
Std HashMap with SipHash was 388 ns/insert; FxBuildHasher dropped to 266 ns (-31%); same pattern as Plan 18-11 D-7 outer state_tables

stashed_work.description: wip: --total-events + pre-encoded-frame bench (stalled after pre-warm; debug next session as start of Phase 19)

next_action: Either (a) resume Phase 18 wrap (Plan 18-06 wire polish then Phase 18 SUMMARY); OR (b) jump to Phase 19 (debug stashed --total-events bench, finalize Rust harness, build Python harness). Recommendation: (b) — 1M EPS measurement is the headline question users ask.
context_notes: M4 saturated EPS final state: 393k -> 522k (+33%) for msgpack TCP small p=16/pd=256. Apply hot path dispatch ~1080 ns/event total. agg substage entity_row_init dominant at ~350 ns (EntityKey hash). bk_evid the 2nd biggest bookkeeping cost at ~200 ns (event_id_index Mutex+insert). Highest-leverage remaining: smaller EntityKey (single-u64 fast path), or skip event_id_index when no descriptor needs retract.
